$description: Pass condition definitions for ACPF and DCPF FNM verification. Machine-readable specification consumed by evaluate-tool agents at runtime.

bus_exclusion.description: Path to the D1 bus exclusion registry. All buses listed in this file are excluded from metric denominators. Exclusion reasons: ide_4_isolated, vm_zero_deenergized, disconnected_island.
bus_exclusion.usage: Load excluded_buses[].bus_number to build the exclusion set. Metric denominators = total_buses - len(exclusion_set).

aggregate.description: Primary pass gate. A bus passes if BOTH VM and VA deviations are within tolerance. The fraction of passing buses must exceed the minimum threshold.

hard_fail.description: Any single condition triggers unconditional test failure, regardless of aggregate statistics.

conditions.name: excessive_failing_fraction
conditions.description: More than 20% of non-excluded buses fail the aggregate tolerance.

conditions.name: extreme_vm_deviation
conditions.description: Any single bus has VM deviation exceeding 0.1 p.u. Indicates fundamental voltage error, not solver variation.

conditions.name: extreme_va_deviation
conditions.description: Any single bus has VA deviation exceeding 10.0 degrees. Indicates topology or connectivity error.

outlier_classification.description: Buses that fail the aggregate tolerance are classified by probable cause. Classification does not change pass/fail -- it explains why outliers exist and whether they indicate ingestion error vs. expected solver variation.
outlier_classification.evaluation_order: Rules are evaluated in the order listed. First matching rule assigns the primary cause. A bus may match multiple rules; only the first (highest priority) is assigned.

rules.description: Bus has a switched shunt device in the intermediate format. Discrete switching step differences between solvers produce VM deviations of 0.002-0.010 p.u. that are legitimate solver-variation artifacts.

Bus has a generator at or near a reactive power limit (|Q - Qmax| < 1.0 MVAr or |Q - Qmin| < 1.0 MVAr in the ACPF reference). Different Q-limit enforcement sequences across solvers produce different voltage setpoints at PV-to-PQ transitioned buses.

Bus is the slack bus (type=3) or within 2 branches of the slack bus in the network graph. Slack bus power absorption differs between solvers, causing VA deviations that propagate to electrically nearby buses.
Bus is the regulated bus (CONT field) of an in-service tap-changing transformer. Different tap optimization algorithms produce different tap positions, causing VM deviations at the regulated bus.
Bus is at the boundary of a weakly connected subnetwork (network degree <= 2 and base_kv < 69 kV). Low-voltage radial boundary buses are highly sensitive to upstream modeling differences.

warning_thresholds.max_classified_description: If classified outliers (all causes except unclassified) exceed 10% of non-excluded buses, emit a warning.
warning_thresholds.max_unclassified_description: If unclassified outliers exceed 2% of non-excluded buses, emit a warning.

formulation_difference_max_abs.description: Maximum absolute VM/VA deviation (p.u.) permitted under formulation_difference classification.

aggregate.description: Primary pass gate. Two independent metrics: bus angles and branch flows.

bus_angle.description: Fraction of non-excluded buses with VA deviation within tolerance.

branch_flow.description: Fraction of in-service branches with P deviation within tolerance.

hard_fail.description: Any single condition triggers unconditional test failure.

conditions.name: excessive_bus_failing_fraction
conditions.description: More than 20% of non-excluded buses fail the VA tolerance.

conditions.name: excessive_branch_failing_fraction
conditions.description: More than 20% of in-service branches fail the P tolerance.

conditions.name: extreme_branch_flow_deviation
conditions.description: Any single branch has P deviation exceeding 50.0%. Indicates topology or impedance error.

formulation_difference_max_abs.description: Maximum absolute VA deviation (degrees) permitted under formulation_difference classification.

voltage_level_tiers.description: Informational voltage-level breakdown in verification results. Not a pass/fail gate -- the primary pass condition uses a single tolerance for all buses. This breakdown helps diagnose systematic voltage-level-correlated errors.
